articles.title: EU-approved pesticide found to have potential effects on brain development

more.title: South Africa introduces mandatory online travel declarations for millions of international travellers
more.description: South Africa has made online traveller declarations mandatory for everyone entering or leaving the country, marking a major step in the government's efforts to digitise customs procedures and strengthen border management.

more.title: South Africa’s 30 June protests leave hundreds arrested as tensions spread nationwide
more.description: A wave of anti-migrant protests swept across South Africa this week amid the 30 June deadline, leading to more than 900 arrests nationwide as tensions over illegal immigration once again spilled onto the streets, Cape {town} Etc reports. While most marches remained peaceful, authorities confirmed that several areas experienced outbreaks of looting, public violence and attacks on foreign-owned businesses, raising fears... Read more → The post South Africa’s 30 June protests leave hundreds arrested as tensions spread nationwide appeared first on Cape Town ETC .

more.title: South African Legal AI Startup Anvaya Wins Local Start-up World Cup, Advances to Silicon Valley Final
more.description: South African legal artificial intelligence startup Anvaya has clinched the top prize at the South African chapter of the Start-up World Cup, earning a place at the competition’s global final in Silicon Valley. The Start-up World Cup, hosted by Pegasus Tech Ventures, is one of the most competitive global platforms for emerging tech startups. With [...]

more.title: Ramaphosa Urges Africa to Accelerate AI Adoption at First Google Cloud Summit on the Continent
more.description: President Cyril Ramaphosa has urged South Africa and the wider African continent to accelerate the adoption of artificial intelligence and cloud technologies, warning that hesitation will leave nations trailing in the global digital race. Delivering the keynote at the first Google Cloud Summit on African soil, held at the Sandton Convention Centre on Wednesday, Ramaphosa [...]

more.title: Anti-illegal immigration activists in KuGompo urge govt to address their grievances
more.description: In the Eastern Cape, police arrested 208 undocumented foreign nationals, while 21 people were arrested for offences linked to the protests.

more.title: Chidimma Adetshina deportation case deepens after Cape Town arrest
more.description: Chidimma Adetshina’s deportation case is set to return to the Cape Town Regional Court on 16 July after the former Miss South Africa finalist and reigning Miss Universe Nigeria was arrested in Summer Greens earlier this month, reports Cape {town} Etc. The Department of Home Affairs alleges that Adetshina and her minor son were living in South Africa without lawful... more.title: EDITH PHASWANA | Migration debate must address the root causes across Africa
more.description: The question we should be asking is, ‘How did we arrive at a point where there are individuals whose identities, statuses, or movements cannot be adequately accounted for?' asks Prof. Edith Phaswana

more.title: Supercomputer predicts unbeaten Springboks to win inaugural Nations Championship
more.description: A supercomputer predicts the Springboks will win the inaugural Rugby Nations Championship unbeaten, with strategic experimentation under coach Rassie Erasmus ahead of the 2027 Rugby World Cup.

more.title: Stellantis reworks Gqeberha plant plans as market shifts
more.description: Stellantis MD Mike Whitfield insists the greenfield project is merely on hold, not abandoned, amid market upheaval.

more.title: Some Malawians exhausted from having to sleep outside: ‘We want to leave’
more.description: The Department of Home Affairs has seen an influx of foreign nationals seeking travel documents that would allow them to cross the Beitbridge border.

more.title: KwaZulu-Natal Protests Remain Mostly Peaceful as Premier Addresses Illegal Migration and Looting Arrests
more.description: KWAZULU-NATAL — The KwaZulu-Natal Premier has confirmed that recent protest marches across the province regarding illegal migration were largely peaceful, despite isolated criminal activities that resulted in ten arrests for attempted looting. Addressing the outcomes of the demonstrations, the provincial leadership detailed ongoing engagements with activists, the relocation of undocumented foreign nationals, and efforts to [...]

more.title: Feared unrest fails to materialise as Eastern Cape protests remain peaceful
more.description: More than 14,000 security personnel deployed ahead of the June 30 anti-immigration demonstrations, but turnout was lower than expected

more.title: South Africa Immigration Protests Trigger Mass Deportations and Raids Amid Economic Crisis
more.description: SOUTH AFRICA — South Africa immigration protests have triggered mass deportations and extensive police raids across the nation, exposing deep-rooted economic crises and widespread frustration over undocumented migration. The recent wave of anti-foreigner demonstrations highlights severe socioeconomic challenges, prompting government crackdowns and border processing operations while revealing a profound disconnect between the public and state institutions. [...]

more.title: Zimbabwe Unveils Comprehensive Reintegration Plan for South Africa Returnees
more.description: HARARE, ZIMBABWE — The Zimbabwean government has officially rolled out a comprehensive Zimbabwe reintegration plan for South Africa returnees, ensuring a safe and dignified homecoming for thousands of nationals displaced by ongoing disturbances in the neighboring country. As of June 26, 2026, approximately 3,624 Zimbabwean nationals have been successfully repatriated and received back into the country. [...]
